## Query: "marksman LSP completion triggerCharacters markdown"

Marksman’s LSP completion trigger characters for Markdown appear to be:

- `[`  
- `#`  
- `(`

This is shown in an LSP initialize/capabilities dump for Marksman: `completionProvider.triggerCharacters` = `["[", "#", "("]` ([Reddit log snippet](https://www.reddit.com/r/HelixEditor/comments/1nol488/markdown_language_server_not_working/)).

Supporting references that Marksman provides Markdown completion features:

- VS Code extension page mentions completion for wiki links/headings ([Visual Studio Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=arr.marksman))
- Sublime package page describes Marksman as a Markdown LSP with completion ([Sublime Text Packages](https://packages.sublimetext.io/packages/LSP-marksman))
- Neovim LSP docs explain that `triggerCharacters` control auto-triggered completion ([Neovim LSP help](https://neo.vimhelp.org/lsp.txt.html))
- IWE docs note Neovim autotrigger fires on the server’s trigger characters ([IWE docs](https://iwe.md/docs/features/autocomplete/))

If you want, I can also show how to read these trigger characters from Marksman inside Neovim, Helix, or VS Code.

## Query: "neovim 0.12 vim.lsp.completion enable default LspAttach autotrigger"

Use an `LspAttach` autocmd and call `vim.lsp.completion.enable(..., { autotrigger = true })` there. In Neovim 0.12, `autotrigger` is **not default**; you must enable it yourself, and it only triggers on the server’s `triggerCharacters` unless you override them ([Neovim LSP docs](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lsp), [lsp.html example](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lsp.html)).  

```lua
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('LspAttach', {
  callback = function(ev)
    local client = vim.lsp.get_client_by_id(ev.data.client_id)
    if client and client:supports_method('textDocument/completion') then
      vim.lsp.completion.enable(true, client.id, ev.buf, {
        autotrigger = true,
      })
    end
  end,
})
```

If you want completion on **every keypress**, Neovim docs say to either extend `client.server_capabilities.completionProvider.triggerCharacters` before enabling completion, or call `vim.lsp.completion.get()` from `InsertCharPre` ([docs](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lsp)).  

Also note:
- This builtin completion path is documented as **Since: 0.12.0** ([lsp.html](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lsp.html)).
- `CTRL-Y` accepts the selected item in the popup menu ([lsp docs](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lsp)).
- `news-0.12` mentions `vim.lsp.completion.enable()` updates in 0.12 ([news-0.12](https://neovim.io/doc/user/news-0.12/)).

If you want, I can give you a version that also forces trigger-on-every-character.

## Query: "blink.cmp enabled markdown filetype still showing completion"

`auto_insert` is not the setting that disables completion. It only controls whether the selected item is inserted automatically. The completion menu can still appear in Markdown.

Use one of these instead:

- Disable blink entirely for Markdown with `enabled`:
```lua
require('blink.cmp').setup({
  enabled = function()
    return vim.bo.filetype ~= 'markdown'
  end,
})
```
Source: [General config](https://cmp.saghen.dev/configuration/general), [Recipes: disable per filetype](https://cmp.saghen.dev/recipes)

- Or keep blink enabled but stop the menu from auto-showing:
```lua
require('blink.cmp').setup({
  completion = {
    menu = {
      auto_show = function(ctx, items)
        return vim.bo.filetype ~= 'markdown'
      end,
    },
  },
})
```
Source: [Completion config](https://cmp.saghen.dev/configuration/completion), [Recipes: disable or delay auto-show](https://cmp.saghen.dev/recipes)

Also relevant: the docs show `auto_insert = function(ctx) return vim.bo.filetype ~= 'markdown' end`, which only affects insertion behavior, not whether completion appears. Source: [Completion list selection](https://cmp.saghen.dev/configuration/reference), [Completion docs](https://cmp.saghen.dev/configuration/completion)

If you want, paste your current `blink.cmp` config and I’ll point to the exact line causing it.

text:
Writing the research brief with findings: vimwiki filetype leaves blink active, which explains the popup.

**Root cause:** The popup is **blink.cmp**, not native `vim.lsp.completion` (never enabled in your config) and not `'autocomplete'` (off in `opts.lua`).

The `Text [Buffer]` / `[LSP]` labels match your blink menu draw config. The markdown disable guard works for `filetype=markdown`, but **vimwiki** (from `extras.lua`) sets most `.md` files to **`filetype=vimwiki`**, so blink stays on. Verified: `README.md` → `vimwiki`, `blink=true`; forced `markdown` → `blink=false`.

**Recommended fix:** Broaden `blink.lua` `enabled` to cover `vimwiki` and `markdown.*`, plus optional `vim.b.completion = false` autocmd. Keep marksman; disable its completion capability only if you still want LSP diagnostics on wiki/markdown buffers.

`/Users/shoe/dotfiles/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/e214c218/research.md`

A targeted autocmd can disable vimwiki and markdown completion without affecting other filetypes. Marksman should remain limited to markdown, since extending it to vimwiki would trigger LSP completion through blink. Most markdown files here are actually vimwiki, so marksman rarely attaches anyway.

The concrete fix disables blink for vimwiki and markdown filetypes by checking vim.bo.filetype directly. A simpler Lua condition can exclude "markdown", "vimwiki", and any markdown variant. Alternatively, an autocmd per blink documentation works. To preserve blink elsewhere, menu.auto_show can be restricted to non-vimwiki buffers.
